agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"None — structured events flow correctly with request IDs propagated"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse writer","status":"broken","issue":"Non-idempotent writes combined with at-least-once delivery guarantees duplicate records in the warehouse. Every retry, every lost ACK, every circuit breaker recovery creates silent data corruption. Analytics queries will over-count."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ exists as a write target but has no consumer, no alerting on depth, and no reprocessing mechanism. Failed events enter a black hole. This negates the resilience benefit of having a DLQ at all."},{"from":"retry mechanism","to":"data warehouse writer","status":"risk","issue":"Exponential backoff retries (3x) multiply the duplicate problem. A transient warehouse timeout that returns after the first retry means 2 copies written. Under sustained partial failures this compounds rapidly."}],"contract_compliance":"The service promises event delivery but cannot promise event correctness. Downstream consumers (dashboards, reports, ML features) will silently consume inflated numbers. There is no contract for exactly-once or deduplication semantics.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The combination of at-least-once delivery, non-idempotent writes, and zero deduplication means the data warehouse is guaranteed to accumulate duplicates under normal operation — not just failure scenarios. Any system reading from this warehouse inherits corrupted counts."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No logging when a duplicate event is written — duplicates are invisible in logs","No log entry for DLQ depth or age of oldest unprocessed message"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong — latency histograms, p99 dashboards, Prometheus integration","gaps":["No metric for duplicate event rate or deduplication hit/miss ratio","No metric for DLQ depth, DLQ age, or DLQ consumer lag (consumer does not exist)","No metric distinguishing first-attempt writes from retry writes — retries look identical to originals"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry distributed tracing with request ID propagation","gaps":["Traces do not tag retry attempts vs original attempts, so duplicate writes are invisible in trace analysis"]},"debugging_capability":"Strong for happy-path issues. Poor for data correctness debugging — an engineer at 3am can trace a single request but cannot answer 'how many duplicates did we write in the last hour' because no instrumentation distinguishes originals from duplicates."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Data warehouse transient timeout during write","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Write succeeds server-side but ACK is lost. Retry writes a duplicate. Add idempotency key (event_id + timestamp hash) to warehouse writes so retries are no-ops."},{"scenario":"Sustained data warehouse outage","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker trips correctly, events route to DLQ. But DLQ has no consumer — events are lost in practice. Build a DLQ consumer with its own retry logic and alert on DLQ depth > 0."},{"scenario":"DLQ fills up during extended outage","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No backpressure or overflow strategy. Define max DLQ size, add alerting at 50%/80%/100% capacity, and implement overflow-to-cold-storage as a safety valve."},{"scenario":"Duplicate events under normal operation (ACK lost)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"This is not a failure scenario — it is normal at-least-once behavior. Warehouse writes MUST be idempotent or a deduplication layer must exist upstream of the warehouse."},{"scenario":"Poison message in event stream","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No mention of poison message handling. A malformed event could cause infinite retry loops. Add max-retry with DLQ routing and schema validation at ingestion."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff, 3 retries. Mechanically sound but dangerous without idempotent writes — each retry is a potential duplicate.","timeout_config":"Not specified in review materials. Needs explicit write timeout to prevent connection pool exhaustion under warehouse slowdown.","circuit_breakers":"Present on warehouse writes. Appropriate pattern. Verify half-open state testing interval and failure threshold are tuned to warehouse SLA.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Circuit breaker + DLQ is the right architecture, but the DLQ is a dead end. Graceful degradation requires eventual recovery, not just failure redirection."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green with load balancer traffic switch. Rollback is instant and well-tested. This is solid.","monitoring_coverage":"Strong for availability and latency. Zero coverage for data correctness — the most critical dimension for an analytics ingestion service.","on_call_documentation":"Needs runbook entries for: (1) How to identify and purge duplicate records from warehouse, (2) How to drain the DLQ manually, (3) How to verify event counts match between ingestion and warehouse, (4) What to do when DLQ depth grows.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/reprocessor service"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add idempotency key column or dedup table in data warehouse","Add DLQ depth alerting threshold configuration"],"infra_provisioning":["DLQ monitoring and alerting setup","Deduplication index or table in data warehouse"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Adding an idempotency key or dedup index to a high-volume analytics table requires careful migration planning. At 10K events/sec the table grows fast — index creation must be online/concurrent to avoid write locks."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent warehouse writes with at-least-once delivery guarantee","reason":"This is not a bug — it is an architectural gap. The service is DESIGNED to produce duplicates (at-least-once) but the warehouse has no mechanism to reject or deduplicate them. Every hour of operation in production silently corrupts analytics data. This requires either: (a) idempotency keys on warehouse writes, or (b) a deduplication layer between ingestion and warehouse. Both require schema changes and new infrastructure.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer or alerting","reason":"The DLQ is marketed as a resilience feature but is functionally a data graveyard. Failed events accumulate with no processing, no alerting, no reprocessing. During an outage, the team will believe events are 'safely queued' when they are actually being silently dropped. A DLQ consumer service must be built and deployed alongside the main service.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No data correctness observability","reason":"For an analytics ingestion service, data correctness IS the product. There are zero metrics for duplicate rate, ingestion-vs-warehouse count reconciliation, or DLQ depth. The on-call engineer can tell you latency is fine while the warehouse silently accumulates millions of duplicate records. Add: duplicate write counter, ingestion/warehouse count reconciliation metric, DLQ depth gauge.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Idempotency gap is a clear architectural defect — not a judgment call","DLQ without consumer is objectively incomplete","Observability for availability is genuinely strong","Blue/green deployment and rollback are production-grade"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact warehouse schema — dedup strategy depends on whether natural keys exist in the event schema","DLQ implementation details — backpressure behavior depends on queue technology (SQS, Kafka, RabbitMQ)","Write timeout configuration — not enough detail provided to assess connection pool risk"],"assumptions":["Load test at 10K events/sec did not measure duplicate rate, only throughput and latency","DLQ is a managed queue service, not an in-memory buffer","Data warehouse supports upsert or unique constraint operations needed for idempotency"]}}
